title: Team Hot Wheels Top Rides Series (2012)
summary: The Team Hot Wheels Top Rides Series was released exclusively at Target stores in 2012. All castings in this series are all 2011 and 2012 mainline releases on a specially designed card by the picks of the four driver teams. Each vehicle comes with a sticker sheet consisting of the Team Hot Wheels logo and the team color icon.

title: World's Smallest Hot Wheels
summary: 200px|rightWorld's Smallest Hot Wheels is a series of Hot Wheels-themed toys manufactured and marketed by Super Impulse as one of their many brands in their World's Smallest product line introduced in 2017. These are approximately 1:180 scale versions of Hot Wheels cars and tracksets; the wheels on the cars turn and the cars run on tracks. Each car is no larger than a quarter.

title: Hot Wheels XL
summary: Hot Wheels XL is a 1:43 scale series of Hot Wheels; in essence the castings in this series are scaled up versions of mainline castings, carrying over their decos and wheels. This series was developed by Charlie Angulo.

title: Hotwheels.com
summary:
center|300px
hotwheels.com was a website first launched in 1997 (domain registered August 21, 1996) that served as a hub for parents, children, and collectors to interact, with the link appearing on various products in the years after. It featured many activities, news posts, webgames, and downloadable content. The site was shut down with its domain redirected to the mattel....

title: List of 2026 Hot Wheels (by Series)
summary: For 2026, the Hot Wheels mainline is numbered 1 through 250 with color variations not receiving a new number. Treasure Hunts and store exclusives are numbered. For the listing by series see List of 2026 Hot Wheels.
